LAW AND MOTION, 302, RE: PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MR. EGAN'S DEMURRER IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR FOR NON-COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THE PARTIES TO CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE FILING. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. IF THE DEMURRER IS RENEWED, IT NEEDS TO BE FILED BY NOVEMBER 9, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS DENIED. ***SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING*** COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAMES EGAN'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM AUTOMATIC STAY DURING L&M'S APPEAL OF THE DENIAL OF ITS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS HELD. MR. ALEXIS ADDRESSES THE COURTESY COPIES. DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION/ARGUMENT, THE COURT VACATED THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 22, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. ON THE LAW & MOTION CALENDAR. COURTESY COPIES ARE TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302. PERSONAL APPEARANCES ARE REQUIRED AT THE NEXT HEARING. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. BAILIFF: PARK. NOT REPORTED. (301/RCE)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MARCH-22-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JAMES EGAN'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM AUTOMATIC STAY DURING L&M'S APPEAL OF THE DENIAL OF ITS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, THE COURT CONTINUES THE MATTER TO APRIL 26, 2024 AT 9:30 A.M. COUNSEL ARE TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH SUPPLEMENTAL LETTER BRIEFS NO LONGER THAN 5 PAGES ADDRESSING CCP 1294 AND THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT. BRIEFS ARE DUE BY APRIL 19, 2024 AND COUNSEL ARE TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH COURTESY COPIES IN COMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7 (B). JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-30-2024 Continued To Aug-21-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew ("Drew") L. Alexis, Esq. of Alexis Law Firm, Tel. 562.544.9809, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff;
Paul Silver, Esq., specially appearing in person for Defendant Lloyd & Mousilli PLLC.

Hearing re: Plaintiff's Motion For Relief From Automatic Stay During L&M'S Appeal Of The Denial Of Its Motion To Compel Arbitration.

The matter is contested. Mr. Silver states that he has not seen the Court's tentative ruling. The matter is passed for Mr. Silver to review the tentative ruling.

At the second calling of the case, argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows with the added language that discovery can resume per the California Code of Civil Procedure:

Plaintiff's motion for relief from the automatic stay is granted.

(See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Bailiff: Jamie; Not Reported. (302/RCE)

Motion For Relief From Automatic Stay During L&M'S Appeal Of The Denial Of Its Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Continued To Apr-26-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. ANDREW ALEXIS, Esq. of Alexis Law Firm (tel: 562-544-9809) for Plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference).
Atty. PAUL A. SILVER, Esq. (tel: 510-701-0171) specially appearing for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF JAMES EGAN's Motion For Relief From Automatic Stay During L&M'S Appeal Of The Denial Of Its Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Matter argued. Upon conclusion of oral argument, the Court continues the matter to April 26, 2024 at 9:30 A.M. Counsel are to provide the Court with supplemental letter briefs no longer than 5 pages addressing CCP 1294 and the Federal Arbitration Act. Briefs are due by April 19, 2024 and Counsel are to provide the Court with courtesy copies in compliance with Local Rule 2.7 (B).

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2023 Continued To Feb-21-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: Defendants' motion to compel arbitration.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Plaintiff demonstrates that he was an employee and therefore, California law applies pursuant to California Labor Code sec. 925. (Egan Decl., pars. 12-16.) Karl v. Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (N.D. Cal. November 6, 2018) 2018 U.S. Dist. LEXIS 189997 at *6-*9 [Labor Code sec. 925 applied where plaintiff alleged facts sufficient to show misclassification claim was "plausible"].) The court rejects defendants' argument that the agreement is subject to a delegation clause because it incorporates the AAA rules. Defendants improperly raise that argument for the first time in the reply brief. (American Indian Model Schools v. Oakland Unified School Dist. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 258, 275 ["We will not ordinarily consider issues raised for the first time in a reply brief."].) In any event, recent authority states that incorporation of the rules is clear and unmistakable only where sophisticated parties are contracting. (Gostev v. Skillz Platform, Inc. (2023) 88 Cal.App.4th 1035, 1049-1053.) The court concludes that the parties' agreement is contradictory and there was no meeting of the minds. Section 14 of the agreement provides that "Any controversy between the Parties to this Agreement involving the construction or application of any of the terms, provisions, or conditions of this Agreement, shall on written request of either party served on the other, be submitted first to mediation and then if still unresolved to binding arbitration." Section 15 states "[t]he courts of Houston, Texas shall be the exclusive forum for any litigation or dispute resolution." The provisions cannot be reconciled and there was no meeting of the minds. The failure to reach a meeting of the minds on all material points prevents the formation of a contract, including an agreement to arbitrate. (See Cheema v. L.S. Trucking, Inc. (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1142, 1149; Banner Entertainment, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 348, 359.) " 'Although the terms of a contract need not be stated in the minutest detail, it ... must evidence a meeting of the minds upon the essential features of the agreement.' "(Cheema, at p. 1149; accord, Weddington Productions, Inc. v. Flick (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 797 ["[i]f no meeting of the minds has occurred on the material terms of a contract, basic contract law provides that no contract formation has occurred"]; see also O'Shaugnessy v. Young Living Essential Oils, LC (W.D. Tex October 18, 2019) 2019 U.S. Dist. LEXIS 180503 (where one clause of contract required "[a]ny legal action concerning the Agreement will be brought in the state and federal courts" while arbitration clause provided that "any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to the Agreement, or the breach thereof, will be settled by arbitration" provisions "irreconcilably conflict" and there was no "meeting of the minds"; Ragh v. Howard (10th Cir. 2016) 841 F. 3d 1134, 1138 ["[T]he conflicting details in the multiple arbitration provisions indicate that there was no meeting of the minds with respect to arbitration."].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Counsel Barazi's application to appear as counsel pro hac vice is granted. The policy in favor of litigants retaining the counsel of their choice outweighs the issues identified in plaintiff's opposition to this application.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing, an order is signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending A Determination On Defendant'S Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant's Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending A Determination On Defendants' Motion To Compel Arbitration.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Denied as moot. The court denied the motion to compel arbitration.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Mr. Egan's demurrer is taken off calendar for non-compliance with CCP 430.41, which requires the parties to conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. If the demurrer is renewed, it needs to be filed by November 9, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
